danger_signals.visual_form: Incoming bullet patterns from airborne and ground units
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat to player ship; collision results in damage/loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Static or moving environmental barriers on ground level
danger_signals.meaning: Collision hazard; ship loss if struck while grounded

danger_signals.visual_form: Large enemy sprite at level end
danger_signals.meaning: Mandatory combat checkpoint; level progression blocked until defeated

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprites in upper air layer
danger_signals.meaning: Danger while player is airborne; avoided by descending to ground

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprites on lower ground platform
danger_signals.meaning: Danger while player is grounded; unavoidable threat on ground level

safety_signals.visual_form: Lower level accessible via button press
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary immunity from airborne attacks; tactical repositioning space
Enhanced firepower and defensive capability; progression requirement

safety_signals.visual_form: Rotating spherical shields attached to ship
safety_signals.meaning: Partial damage reduction; active defense mechanism

safety_signals.visual_form: Six radial projectiles forming close barrier

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: FX-1 ship sprite; winged configuration when armed
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears in both air and ground layers; central screen position

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Varied enemy sprite designs per level; Gobunasu boss forms
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Arcade vs. Famicom versions feature different enemy appearances and attack patterns
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Distributed across air and ground layers; approaching from edges or below

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ship sprite without wing attachments; single projectile fire
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced offensive capability; baseline damage output only

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Wing power-up attached to ship sides; expanded sprite silhouette
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enhanced firepower pattern based on equipped weapon type; improved progression capability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ship in upper half of screen; unaffected by ground obstacles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Immunity to ground unit attacks; vulnerable to airborne enemies; automatic height reset after duration

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ship descends to lower platform; dual-layer screen visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Immunity to airborne attacks; vulnerability to ground unit fire; can still collide with obstacles; time-limited stay before forced ascent

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Equipped wing sprite shows deterioration/partial destruction
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Weapon auto-ejects upon sufficient damage; returns to wingless state; firepower reduced during degradation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gobunasu boss sprite appears; level end zone reached
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mandatory combat engagement; standard movement blocked; boss attack patterns differ per level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Background image continues infinitely in horizontal directions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can scroll left/right without boundary limits; maintains engagement area throughout traversal

environmental_storytelling.example: Two distinct visual planes represent different tactical spaces; ground level provides visual evidence of escape mechanic and obstacle navigation challenge
Wing-form power-ups communicate that player advancement is achieved through equipment collection; varied weapon designs suggest different strategic approaches available
Unique Gobunasu designs per level end communicate escalating threat and visual progression through game world
Ground platform obstacles telegraph difficulty curve; increased concentration signals approaching challenge peaks

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Fires single projectile; maximum vulnerability; baseline movement speed
Fires three rapid projectiles; machine-gun effect; enhanced forward pressure
Fires five spreading projectiles; auto-despawn on hit; defensive spread formation
Ignores ground-level unit attacks; forced descent after time limit; optimal for airborne engagements
Immune to airborne attacks; vulnerable to ground units; collision hazard with obstacles present
Attack player when airborne; ignored when player is grounded; approach from edges and above
Attack player regardless of position; ground-level threat persistent; melee or projectile attack patterns
Unique attack pattern per level; mandatory defeat to progress; larger sprite indicates significance
Degradation visible on sprite; auto-ejects upon critical damage; forces weapon re-acquisition

notes: B-Wings uses a dual-layer spatial design (air/ground) as its primary world-state communication mechanism. The game lacks narrative exposition or dialogue; all world-state understanding derives from mechanical feedback and visual form. Weapon systems function as both progression gates and visual identity markers. The Famicom version introduces additional visual complexity through new weapon types (Hammer shields, Dyna beam, Fire jet) not present in arcade, suggesting world-state divergence between versions. No faction narrative or political context is provided; enemies are generic threatening entities without allegiance or motivation storytelling. Environmental storytelling is minimal—the game prioritizes pure mechanical communication over narrative world-building.
